window_fidelity.evidence: clean_iso: four glazed windows on exterior walls (two framed front-facade windows + two small awning windows) plus the terrace sliding glass; count matches windows3d=4. All seated within exterior walls, none floating or full-height voids.

door_fidelity.evidence: Six door swings visible in clean_top, each hinged on a wall (hosted, none floating). The central pier near image center carries two swings and clean_iso shows a tight cluster of brown door leaves there — dense but seated on walls, no leaf detached in space. Positions correspond to PDF suite/bath/entry doors.

room_fidelity.evidence: Every room carries a distinct pastel floor fill fully contained within its walls in clean_top — no color bleeding into the gray exterior (no floor leak). Room set and placement (kitchen, dining, living, suites, bath, terrace) correspond to the PDF layout in clean_side_by_side.

wall_fidelity.evidence: clean_top: outer perimeter reads as continuous dark wall volumes with a stepped facade/terrace profile that matches the PDF; no erased segments, no open perimeter. Interior partitions are present and fully enclose every colored room; the stepped notches at bottom-left/bottom-right are wall-thickness/column steps, not gaps.

scale_rotation.evidence: clean_side_by_side: the SKP top view matches the PDF footprint proportions and orientation (long horizontal body with left terrace step and right rooms); no mirroring, rotation, or scale mismatch.

global_visual.evidence: Clean render on uniform gray background; no floating geometry, orphan glass panels, or artifacts. Model reads as one coherent, fully enclosed floor plan consistent with the PDF.

window_fidelity.evidence: clean_iso shows ~4 glazed openings in the facade walls (two 2-pane sliders plus small awning units, distinct from the balcony railing glass), consistent with windows3d=4 and the PDF window positions.

door_fidelity.evidence: Multiple brown door swings are each seated in a wall opening; the small central pier hosts two adjacent bathroom doors, consistent with the PDF's BANHEIRO/LAVABO cluster. No clearly hostless/floating leaf observed.

room_fidelity.evidence: Each room carries a distinct floor fill (yellow, cyan, green, pink, lilac) and reads as an enclosed zone matching the PDF room partition; no floor color bleeds past its bounding walls.

wall_fidelity.evidence: In corrupted_top the upper (facade) wall of the central cyan living room is not continuous: a short dark wall segment floats near the top-center (approx between the entry door and the small central room) with open gaps on either side, so the room's top perimeter does not close cleanly. Reads as an erased/broken wall run rather than a windowed wall.

scale_rotation.evidence: In corrupted_side_by_side the SKP top and iso align with the PDF's proportions and orientation (L-shaped mass, terrace bottom-left, suites right); no visible rotation or scale drift.

global_visual.evidence: Overall massing, room layout and openings track the PDF closely; the single broken top-wall segment is the only structural deviation from an otherwise faithful model.

findings.location: top/facade wall of the central cyan living room, upper-center (between the entry door swing and the small central room)
findings.evidence: The top perimeter wall of the central living room is discontinuous: a short isolated wall segment floats near the top with open gaps on both sides, leaving the room's upper edge non-enclosed. Appears as an erased/gapped wall run, not a thin-rendered window.
